payment.title: Payment Guide
payment.subtitle: Complete guide for payments in China
payment.alipayDesc: China's dominant mobile payment platform, accepted everywhere.
payment.wechatPayDesc: Integrated with messaging app, second most popular payment.
payment.touristCardDesc: Prepaid card designed for tourists, easy setup without bank account.
payment.cashDesc: Still useful for small vendors, markets, and emergencies.
payment.bankCardDesc: International credit/debit cards work at ATMs and most merchants.
payment.marketTipDesc: Bargaining is expected at markets. Start at 30-40% of asking price.
payment.taxRefundDesc: You can get VAT refund on purchases over 500 CNY at participating stores.
payment.atmTip: Use ICBC, Bank of China, or HSBC ATMs for best service.
payment.securityWarningDesc: Never share your payment passwords, verification codes, or personal information with strangers.
payment.faq1: Do I need a Chinese bank account for mobile payments?
payment.faq1Answer: Foreign credit cards now work with Alipay and WeChat Pay, but with limitations. For full features, a Chinese bank account is helpful but not always required.
payment.faq2: Is it safe to use mobile payments in China?
payment.faq2Answer: Yes, mobile payments in China are very secure. Alipay and WeChat have millions of users with strong security measures. Use biometric verification for large transactions.
payment.faq3: How much cash should I bring to China?
payment.faq3Answer: For emergencies, carry 500-1000 CNY equivalent in local currency. Most urban places accept mobile payment. Rural areas may need more cash.
